metadata.title:
Spelling PLACES
Of BEAUTY


HERE

Writer Says Straight Line
Engineers Spelling City's


Natural Advantages.

pages.raw_text:
That Spokane is thoroughly en-
titled to it's claim as a City Beau
tiful' is agreed by one writer
whose article appears in the
Twentieth Century magazine this
month. Much space is given in
the magazine to enthusiastic com
ment on the city's future prospects
past success and present beauty
and elegant half page halftones OF
the principal buildings, parks and
institutions take up a half dozen
nicely arranged pages of the popu
lar little book. Pictures of the
Masonic temple, the court house,
South Central high School building,
Christian Science church and of
Mantle and Liberty parks, and two
splendid pictures taken from diff
ferent points of vantage through
Occur d'Alene park, are shown.


|. OF the city of Spokane, the ar
ticle in part is as follows:


INTELLECTUALLY, Spokane is far
ahead of any city of it's size with
which / am familiar. Both in bust
ness blocks and residences it is
deserving of high praise. There is
little of the bizarre or weakly pre-
tentiOus as one so often finds in
the architecture of new towns. Ev-
erything is solid, substantial, digni-
fled and harmonious. A glimpse
down Riverside avenue reveals a
score of business blocks that would
demand nttnnfjNn in L.
Boston or any other large eastern
city. Well designed and equally
well built, they reveal business
prosperity as well as architectural
taste. Some of the buildings rise
far above the average, and the
churches are as artistically pleas
ing as are the business blocks.


"As usual, the real estate man
and the "straight-line engineer" did
their best to spoil the natural adj
vantages of the growing city. No
provision was made for breathing
places, in the form of city squares
i'll the business section; and to-
wards the west the possibilities OF
the riverfrOnt were totally ignored
Its scenic banks below the falls
were made dumping places for
trash of every kind, or the site OF
shacks that shock the eye and Of
fend the taste. They are naturally
adapted for the most beautiful of
homes and small parks where all
the people could come at allStimes
and enjoy the flowing of theIi4er
the CLINT OF the sun upon a tree.
lined stream, and the exquisite
glories and delights of the falls
above. Who does not know the
charm and fascination that Niagara
possesses for thousands and thous.
ands OF people from all parts of
the world, who come to it again
and again to revel in the various
colors, the changing tints and
shades, the fume, the fret, the
majesty and the mystery that are
always felt in the roar of a. great
waterfall? And only in a lesser
degree than Niagara, the Spokane
falls appeal to the same emotior/
and produce the same sensations
of delight and pleasure. To rescue
these river banks and provide
sight seeing and scenic breathing
spots is one of the endeavors OF
the present park board, and to con
meet them with a well arranged
and beautiful civic center and q
system of parks and scenic reser
vatlons commensurate with the
citys commercial and financial i'm
| pOrtance."
